task_completion.description: Navigate to the settings menu where distance rings are configured.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Avare settings or preferences screen that contains options for distance rings.

task_completion.description: Ensure the distance rings are enabled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A checkbox, toggle, or list option for enabling distance rings is visibly checked or turned on in the settings.

task_completion.description: Set the inner ring distance to 5.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box, text input field, or settings summary explicitly shows the inner ring value set to '5'.

task_completion.description: Set the outer ring distance to 10.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box, text input field, or settings summary explicitly shows the outer ring value set to '10'.

task_completion.description: Ensure the rings are visible on the map view.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The final screenshots show the main Avare map interface with two concentric distance rings visible around the user's location or center point.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the Avare GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show standard Android UI interactions within the Avare app, not a terminal or text editor.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not cheat by manually editing the preferences XML file.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no screenshots of terminal commands or text editors modifying '/data/data/com.ds.avare/shared_prefs/com.ds.avare_preferences.xml'.

integrity.description: Verify the final map view is the actual application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The map view showing the rings must be the live Avare application interface, complete with its standard UI overlays and menus, not an image viewer or gallery app.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target inner ring distance is exactly 5, and the target outer ring distance is exactly 10. The configuration is saved in the standard Android shared preferences for com.ds.avare, but the VLM must verify the configuration visually through the app's GUI settings and the final map view.
